2011-03-27.abstract: There were unconfirmed reports early Monday that rebel forces had routed pro-government defenders in Col. Muammar el-Qaddafi’s tribal homeland of Surt.

2011-03-28.title: Gates and Clinton Unite to Defend Libya Intervention, and Say It May Last Awhile
2011-03-28.abstract: The comments by President Obama’s two top national security officials offered a striking illustration of the complex calculus involved in imposing a no-fly zone over Libya.

2011-03-28.title: Libyans Call Woman Who Claimed Gang Rape a Prostitute
2011-03-28.abstract: Libyan authorities attacked the character and credibility of a woman who claimed that she had been abducted and raped by militia members working for Col. Muammar el-Qaddafi.

2011-03-28.title: For $1,000, Site Lets Celebrities Say It Ain’t So
2011-03-28.abstract: A new Web site called ICorrect allows people who are unhappy about how they are portrayed on the Internet to set the record straight for $1,000 a year.

2011-03-28.title: In Deference to Crisis, a New Obsession Sweeps Japan: Self-Restraint
2011-03-28.abstract: With hundreds of thousands of people displaced and many still missing, anything with the barest hint of luxury invites condemnation in post-tsunami Japan.

2011-03-28.title: Quake Area Residents Turn to Old Means of Communication to Keep Informed
2011-03-28.abstract: A nation where many people were accustomed to instantaneous access to information has reached into the past for more tried-and-true means of communication.

2011-03-29.title: Clashes Fuel Debate Over U.S. Plan to Leave Iraq
2011-03-29.abstract: Ethnic violence in Kirkuk has led some to believe that civil war could result if the United States sticks to its schedule.

2011-03-29.title: Rebels Halted Outside Surt
2011-03-29.abstract: Rebel forces’ westward charge was repulsed by fire from forces guarding one of Col. Muammar el-Qaddafi’s most crucial bastions of support.

2011-03-29.title: Porn Stars, Clad? They Seem to Appeal to Indonesian Filmgoers
2011-03-29.abstract: In a country with strict laws against pornography, Maxima Pictures has stumbled on a successful formula.

2011-03-29.title: Marine Life Faces Threat From Runoff
2011-03-29.abstract: High levels of radioactive cesium have been detected near the Fukushima Daiichi nuclear power plant in Japan, raising concerns that radiation could enter the food chain.

2011-03-29.title: Obama Cites Limits of U.S. Role in Libya
2011-03-29.abstract: President Obama defended the military assault in Libya, saying it was in America’s national interest to stop a potential massacre.

2011-03-30.title: Lessons From Fukushima Taught on Capitol Hill
2011-03-30.abstract: Nuclear safety experts spoke at a Senate committee briefing that examined potential improvements at American plants.

2011-03-30.title: Syria’s Cabinet Resigns; Concessions Expected
2011-03-30.abstract: The resignation came ahead of a speech President Bashar al-Assad is expected to give to the nation on Wednesday that may offer other concessions.

2011-03-30.title: Washington in Fierce Debate on Arming Libyan Rebels
2011-03-30.abstract: Fears that providing arms would pull the United States into a civil war, as well as concerns that some fighters may have links to Al Qaeda, have spawned fierce debate.

2011-03-30.title: Zimbabwe Lawmakers Pick Mugabe Rival for Post
2011-03-30.abstract: Lovemore Moyo, of the Movement for Democratic Change, had been elected speaker of the country’s Parliament in 2008, but his victory was invalidated three weeks ago on a legal technicality.

2011-03-30.title: China: Government Tells U.N. Agency Not to Interfere
2011-03-30.abstract: A Chinese Foreign Ministry spokeswoman said human rights monitors at the United Nations should respect China’s sovereignty.

2011-03-31.title: In Yemen, Sit-In Against the Government Yields Business Opportunities
2011-03-31.abstract: Entrepreneurs are stepping in to serve protesters who say they intend to demonstrate until President Ali Abdullah Saleh goes.

2011-03-31.title: Elections in Egypt by the Fall, Leaders Say
2011-03-31.abstract: Voting for president and a new constitution would be important steps in getting military rulers out of a governing role.

2011-03-31.title: Islamists Are Elated by Revolts, Cleric Says
2011-03-31.abstract: A top propagandist for Al Qaeda said in a video posted on the Web that Islamists had been elated by the protests around the Arab world.

2011-03-31.title: Captured by Qaddafi's Forces
2011-03-31.abstract: On March 15, four New York Times journalists covering the Libyan conflict were captured. They were freed six days later. Back in New York, they reflected on Col. Muammar el-Qaddafi's Libya.

2011-03-31.title: Libyans Offer Credible Case of Death by Airstrike
2011-03-31.abstract: An 18-month-old baby is the first specific and credible civilian death from the airstrikes that the Qaddafi government has presented in 10 days of official statements.
